title: Paradoxically, I feel like vibe coding has made me MORE creative, by pushing me to constantly ask "What are some things a person *with a different skillset than me* can solve with just a little… | Hillel Wayne
author: Hillel Wayne
text:
Paradoxically, I feel like vibe coding has made me MORE creative, by pushing me to constantly ask "What are some things a person *with a different skillset than me* can solve with just a little programming?" It also makes me learn more, because when the LLM goes off track (as it often does), I always find it easier to just debug and fix the problem myself. That means learning some of the skillset. I should probably try getting real drunk and vibe coding a bunch of scripts, just to see what ideas for programs a chemically-altered brain comes up with. But not actually run them, of course, because I'm not an idiot.
Weed. Weed vibe coding.
According to science, that might work https://time.com/4989850/alcohol-foreign-language-speak/
This is it. I am some much more confident to do PoCs the right way instead of the sloppy way.
Smoked coding it is :)
